scope: Ros2_Slam host-side bridge timing and launch defaults
Clion firmware control loop, timeout, and transport timing
Cross-repo documentation alignment for timing and heading defaults

static_findings.title: Host cmd_timeout exceeded MCU timeout window after keepalive refresh
static_findings.rationale: At 20 Hz the host loop runs every 50 ms. With 0.25 s cmd_timeout and 0.10 s keepalive, the bridge emits an explicit zero command no later than the 300 ms MCU timeout window instead of extending it.

static_findings.title: Firmware protocol document still claimed use_status_yaw=false as current host default
static_findings.rationale: The actual host default has already been use_status_yaw=true. Documentation was updated to match code.

static_verification: bash -n launch_scripts/robot.sh launch_scripts/save_map.sh launch_scripts/start_mapping.sh launch_scripts/start_navigation.sh launch_scripts/start_nav_fake.sh launch_scripts/stop_all.sh launch_scripts/lib/common.sh
rg scan for stale timing and use_status_yaw defaults across Ros2_Slam and Clion
residual_risks: Static audit cannot prove real serial jitter, ACK loss bursts, or navigation runtime behavior under repeated goal cancellation.
Real robot validation is still required for cmd_vel to odom closure, repeated navigation, and narrow-space planning behavior.
